name.fr: Couronne des Rois mexicaine (Rosca de Reyes)

description.en: A butter-rich, orange-scented ring baked for Día de Reyes on January 6th, its crown set with strips of candied guava paste and glacé cherries like jewels. A tiny hidden figure of the Christ child makes whoever finds it host tamales on Candlemas.
description.de: Ein buttriger, orangenduftender Hefekranz für den 6. Januar, das Dreikönigsfest: seine Krone ist mit Streifen kandierter Guavenpaste und Glasurkirschen wie Edelsteinen besetzt. Wer die versteckte Jesusfigur findet, richtet an Mariä Lichtmess die Tamales aus.
description.es: Una rosca de pan dulce, mantequillosa y perfumada de naranja, para el Día de Reyes el 6 de enero: su corona luce tiras de ate de guayaba y cerezas cristalizadas como joyas. A quien le toque el muñequito escondido le tocará ofrecer los tamales en la Candelaria.
description.fr: Une couronne briochée au beurre et à l'orange pour le Jour des Rois, le 6 janvier : parée de bandes de pâte de goyave confite et de cerises confites, comme des joyaux. Qui trouve la figurine cachée devra offrir les tamales à la Chandeleur.

origin.region: Nationwide Mexico; a central-Mexico holiday custom above all
origin.note: Descends from Spain's Roscón de Reyes, brought over by Spanish colonizers; that tradition in turn traces to older European midwinter fruit-studded ring cakes with roots reaching back to Roman Saturnalia and Twelfth Night customs.

label.fr: bandes de pâte de goyave confite

yield.shape: large ring, about 30 cm across, serves 10-12

keeping.note: Best the day it's baked; wrap airtight and it stays soft about three days, or freeze the plain, undecorated ring.

method.title: Mix the dough
method.summary: Combine flour with the yeast and salt on opposite sides, then work in sugar, butter, eggs, milk, orange zest and orange extract until a soft, slightly sticky dough forms with no dry flour left.

method.title: Knead
method.summary: Knead on a floured surface for 10-15 minutes until smooth and elastic enough to stretch thin without tearing.

method.title: First rise
method.summary: Cover and let the dough rise somewhere warm until nearly doubled, about 1 hour 45 minutes.

method.title: Shape the ring
method.summary: Deflate the dough, form it into a ball, then poke a hole through the center and stretch it out into a wide ring on a lined baking sheet.

method.title: Decorate and proof
method.summary: Brush the ring with beaten egg, lay strips of candied guava paste and glacé cherries over the top like jewels, then let it proof, covered, until springy, about 45 minutes.

method.title: Bake
method.summary: Bake starting hot, around 200°C (400°F), for 10 minutes, then lower the heat and continue until deep golden and the center reads close to 88°C, roughly 13 more minutes.

method.title: Cool and hide the figure
method.summary: Let the ring cool completely, then tuck a small figurine of the Christ child up into the crumb from underneath before serving.

source.adaptation: Converted from cup measurements. Kept the classic candied-fruit topping (guava paste, glacé cherries) but omitted the source's added shortbread decoration paste for a simpler, more traditional finish.
